sourceMovie.title: Alexander
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Alexander (2004) stuck because it commits to its war premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Oliver Stone's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The war and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Flyboys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alexander clicked for you, Flyboys (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and history, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Kingdom of Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alexander clicked for you, Kingdom of Heaven (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The General
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alexander clicked for you, The General (1926) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Alexander?
faq.answer: Try Flyboys, Kingdom of Heaven, Braveheart—each shares DNA with Alexander while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Alexander?
faq.answer: Alexander (2004) is best described as War, History, Action, Adventure, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Alexander worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this war lane.
